findings.summary: In the mundane internal emails the sender routinely refers to himself or herself by full name in the third person, which no real author writing their own note would do.
findings.evidence: d:0030 is from Sandra Mcdonald yet reads 'I have listed Sandra Mcdonald so their notices come to me' (first person and third-person self-name in one sentence). d:0057 is from Christopher Walton: 'Christopher Walton is reachable by phone for a real emergency only.' Same tell in d:0016 (Julie Hill: 'Julie Hill is reachable by phone'), d:0033 (Julie Hill: 'reach Julie Hill by phone'), and d:0037 (John Macias: 'Route anything you cannot resolve to John Macias'). A person writes 'I'm reachable' / 'route it to me', not their own name.

findings.summary: A status report prepared by John Macias switches between first person and naming himself in the third person within a single closing paragraph.
findings.evidence: d:0073 (Prepared by John Macias) closes: 'I will keep this report on the same rhythm ... Anything that needs a decision from your side, John Macias will bring to you directly and early rather than let it sit.' The 'I' and 'John Macias' are the same person one sentence apart.
